message:
chore(beads): close bd-21ypi (c11threads workspace gate green, fix in 138c07c3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-20s","title":"Kernel: Proof-carrying policy tables (implement loader + hash verifier)","description":"Implement runtime artifact loader/verifier.

Requirements:
- No file I/O on hot path; artifact embedded or initialized once.
- Deterministic parsing and verification.

Deliverables:
- policy_table.rs (or proof_policy.rs) + tests.
- Summary exposes active policy hash/version.","notes":"Wiring + build validation for bd-20s:
- Enabled proof-carrying policy table loader to compile by ensuring workspace deps (`blake3`, `sha2`) and membrane deps are present.
- `crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/policy_table.rs` compiles + unit tests pass.
- Ran: `cargo test -p glibc-rs-membrane` and `cargo clippy -p glibc-rs-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings` (both OK).

Next: coordinate commit/landing cleanly given other in-flight worktree changes (bd-3dv).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoEagle","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:33.769594698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:50:15.102197164Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:50:15.102167439Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20s","depends_on_id":"bd-2dz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20s","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":710,"issue_id":"bd-20s","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete: policy_table.rs (854 lines) with PCPT v1 format parser, BLAKE3/SHA-256 dual-hash verification, deterministic parsing, safety invariant checks (strict-no-repair, risk monotonicity), 6 passing tests. Module declared in mod.rs, blake3/sha2 deps in Cargo.toml. All deliverables met.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:38:03Z"},{"id":711,"issue_id":"bd-20s","author":"FuchsiaHollow","text":"Verified complete. Wired policy_table module into runtime_math, added blake3+sha2 workspace deps, fixed clippy warnings. 6/6 tests pass. Delivers: verify_pcpt(), VerifiedPolicyTable, PolicyTableSummary, key_v1_index, risk/budget/consistency bucketizers, risk monotonicity + mode refinement invariant checks. Unblocks bd-3kh.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:39:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-215","title":"Harness: RaptorQ evidence decode integration (command + proof output)","description":"Wire the offline decoder into harness CLI.

Inputs:
- Exported evidence symbol records from runtime.

Outputs:
- Decoded evidence payload.
- Decode proof / failure reasons when decode fails.
- Deterministic formatting via frankentui.

Note:
- Decoder implementation lives outside libc runtime; may use asupersync::raptorq.","notes":"Implemented as part of bd-pc4 closure.

- Harness CLI now has `decode-evidence` subcommand (see `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/bin/harness.rs`).
- Decoder + proof types live in `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/evidence_decode.rs`.
- Deterministic render lives in `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/evidence_decode_render.rs` (plain + ftui via `frankentui-ui`).
- Evidence record parsing + hash-chain verification helpers added to `crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

Command:
- `cargo run -p glibc-rs-harness --bin harness -- decode-evidence --input <file> --format json|plain|ftui`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoEagle","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.257395221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:25:23.071216027Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:25:23.071194727Z","close_reason":"Wired offline evidence decoder into harness CLI with deterministic proof output","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-215","depends_on_id":"bd-pc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-21k","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Deterministic condvar integration fixtures (strict+hardened)","description":"Background:
- Condvar quality depends on integration behavior under contention and mixed clock/time conditions.

Goal:
- Build integration fixture pack for pthread_cond_* with deterministic replay and deep diagnostics.

Deliverables:
1) Producer/consumer scenarios with bounded queues.
2) Timeout scenarios for both supported clocks.
3) Stress scenarios with many waiters and mixed signaling patterns.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Fixtures pass strict+hardened with stable outcomes.
- Failure outputs include enough detail to debug wake/timeout ordering.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E scripts with strict/hardened split and seed controls.
- Structured logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, waiter_count, signal_count, timeout_count, outcome.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.628792480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:12:31.002136199Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:12:31.002057752Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","logging","pthread","testing"],"comments":[{"id":712,"issue_id":"bd-21k","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed: 4 deterministic condvar integration scenarios implemented and passing (68 total condvar tests). Created pthread_condvar_integration_scenarios.v1.json with 8 scenario specs. Scenarios: producer_consumer_bounded_queue, stress_many_waiters_mixed_wake, timedwait_monotonic_past_deadline, signal_before_wait_not_queued. All run in both strict+hardened modes. cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:17Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-21ypi","title":"[HIGH] review: workspace all-targets gate is red in c11threads ABI suite","description":"[HIGH] review: workspace all-targets gate is red in c11threads ABI suite

Observed on current `main` via remote worker:
- Command: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- Result: exit 101 in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Deterministic failing tests:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`
- `test_mtx_multi_threaded_contention`
- `test_cnd_wait_signal`
- `test_cnd_broadcast_wakes_all`

Current symptoms:
- `thrd_join` returns `THRD_ERROR` instead of `THRD_SUCCESS` and/or fails to propagate the thread return value.
- mutex contention test loses increments (`304` vs expected `400`).
- condition-variable signal/broadcast only wakes 0/1 waiters instead of all expected waiters.

Likely investigation points:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-124` (`thrd_create` / `thrd_join` trampoline and retval bridge)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:242-343` (`mtx_lock`, `cnd_broadcast`, `cnd_wait` wrappers)
- the underlying pthread implementation used by those wrappers

This is blocking release confidence because the full workspace test gate is currently red.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:26:50.873600164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:56.698665733Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","regression","review","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-21ypi","title":"[HIGH] review: workspace all-targets gate is red in c11threads ABI suite","description":"[HIGH] review: workspace all-targets gate is red in c11threads ABI suite

Observed on current `main` via remote worker:
- Command: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- Result: exit 101 in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Deterministic failing tests:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`
- `test_mtx_multi_threaded_contention`
- `test_cnd_wait_signal`
- `test_cnd_broadcast_wakes_all`

Current symptoms:
- `thrd_join` returns `THRD_ERROR` instead of `THRD_SUCCESS` and/or fails to propagate the thread return value.
- mutex contention test loses increments (`304` vs expected `400`).
- condition-variable signal/broadcast only wakes 0/1 waiters instead of all expected waiters.

Likely investigation points:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-124` (`thrd_create` / `thrd_join` trampoline and retval bridge)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:242-343` (`mtx_lock`, `cnd_broadcast`, `cnd_wait` wrappers)
- the underlying pthread implementation used by those wrappers

This is blocking release confidence because the full workspace test gate is currently red.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:26:50.873600164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:27:54.664724292Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:27:54.662311690Z","close_reason":"Fix already landed in commit 138c07c3 (pthread_join delegates to host glibc for non-managed handles when force_native_threading is off). Independent investigation reached the same diagnosis and patch; re-running the c11threads_abi_test suite on main now shows 36 passed / 0 failed. Agent collision — cod shipped first, our local re-derivation was a no-op.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","regression","review","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-226","title":"Full release rehearsal: clean-checkout gate run + complete artifact dossier","description":"Background:
- Final confidence requires a full rehearsal from clean checkout through all mandatory gates and artifact publication.

Goal:
- Execute and codify a deterministic release rehearsal that proves the system is operationally complete.

Deliverables:
1) One-command release rehearsal script/pipeline using canonical gate order.
2) Full artifact bundle generation and validation.
3) Failure classification matrix and operator runbook.
4) Final sign-off report template with objective acceptance fields.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Rehearsal succeeds on clean environment with no manual patching.
- Rehearsal failure modes are deterministic and actionable.
- Output contains complete evidence required by closure policy.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for report assembly/validation components.
- E2E rehearsal tests with both success and expected-failure scenarios.
- Structured logs across the whole run with gate-level timings and artifact refs.

Porting + Optimization + Alien Requirements:
- Rehearsal must include spec conformance evidence, performance proof artifacts, and runtime decision explainability artifacts in one unified package.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.814694447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T04:28:10.677816486Z","close_reason":"Release rehearsal: scripts/ci.sh, scripts/check_replacement_levels.sh, tests/release/dossier_validation_report.v1.json, LD_PRELOAD smoke ds_on_id":"bd-30o.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-33p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-3ot.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-226","depends_on_id":"bd-kan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-22p","title":"One-lever discipline guard: prevent multi-lever optimization commits","description":"Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Add process guardrail (template/check) requiring one optimization lever per bead/commit.
- Track lever category in optimization metadata.

Acceptance:
- Each optimization bead references exactly one lever category.
- Multi-lever submissions are auto-flagged for split.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.362012683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:20:25.633743Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:20:25.633743Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf","process"],"comments":[{"id":713,"issue_id":"bd-22p","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Discipline spec: tests/conformance/one_lever_discipline.json
- 8 lever categories: stub_elimination, callthrough_removal, simd_acceleration, cache_optimization, dispatch_optimization, allocation_optimization, syscall_optimization, subsystem_implementation
- Each category has description and examples
- Enforcement rules: one lever per bead, multi-lever requires waiver

### 2. Opportunity matrix updated: tests/conformance/opportunity_matrix.json
- Added lever_category field to all 12 entries
- All entries reference exactly one valid category
- No multi-lever beads detected

### 3. Gate script: scripts/check_one_lever_discipline.sh
- 4 checks: spec exists, matrix exists, all entries have valid lever_category, no multi-lever beads

### 4. Harness tests: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/one_lever_discipline_test.rs
- 7 tests: spec_exists_and_valid, categories_have_required_fields, summary_consistent, all_entries_have_valid_lever_category, no_multi_lever_beads_without_waiver, gate_script_exists_and_executable, taxonomy_covers_standard_types

### Test commands
```
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test one_lever_discipline_test  # 7 pass
bash scripts/check_one_lever_discipline.sh                       # PASS
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness                                   # 220+ pass, 0 failures
```","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:20:25.633743Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gtf.5","title":"L2 partial-replace battery + standalone dependency policy gate","description":"Background:
- L2 requires a subset standalone path without hidden host-libc dependencies.

Scope:
- Define L2 program battery and verify bootstrap/threading/NSS/locale/iconv prerequisites for declared subset.
- Add explicit exclusion list for out-of-scope dynamic features.

Deliverables:
1) L2 subset battery specification.
2) Dependency/policy checks for standalone claims.
3) L2 pass/fail artifact template.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Declared L2 subset runs deterministically without forbidden dependencies.
- Exclusions are explicit and test-verified.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for standalone policy checks.
- E2E L2 subset program runs.
- Logs: trace_id, level=L2, program, dependency_scan, outcome, exclusion_reason.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-gtf.6","title":"L3 full-replace claim-control gate tied to prerequisite completion","description":"Background:
- L3 full replacement claim has the highest compatibility and risk burden.

Scope:
- Define L3 dynamic-program battery and claim-control gate requiring completion of hard-parts, symbol coverage, and no-forbidden call-through conditions.
- Enforce claim immutability unless evidence set is complete.

Deliverables:
1) L3 claim gate specification.
2) Dynamic-program battery requirements.
3) Claim-control policy integrated into release process.

Acceptance Criteria:
- L3 claim blocked unless all prerequisite gates are satisfied.
- Any drift after claim invalidates release readiness.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for claim-control logic.
- E2E L3 candidate runs on dynamic programs.
- Logs: trace_id, level=L3, prerequisite_status, claim_decision, blocking_reasons.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.978447649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:16:38.699124611Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:16:38.698922589Z","close_reason":"Implemented the L3 release-claim gate slice in the release dossier flow: added release_notes_hook output to the validator, hardened FLC_RELEASE_DOSSIER_RELEASE_NOTES_LIMIT parsing with warning+default fallback, and extended frankenlibc-harness release_dossier_validator_test coverage for closed-bead selection and invalid-limit handling. -{"id":"bd-gwqja","title":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread trampoline spins unbounded","description":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread start trampoline spins without a bound while waiting for handoff

The host-thread bootstrap path contains an unbounded busy-wait:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1390-1399` loops on `start_ctx.host_thread.load(Ordering::Acquire)` until the parent stores a non-zero thread id.
- the parent only performs that store after `host_create` returns and after writing the thread id back to the caller (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1231-1252` and `1356-1380`).

Under normal scheduling this window is short, but there is no bound, no backoff, and no fallback park. If the creator thread is delayed under load, preempted in a bad spot, or stuck before the store, the child burns CPU in a pure spin loop.

That is a resource-bounds risk rather than a functional mismatch: it can turn thread creation pressure into avoidable CPU amplification.

Fix direction:
- replace the open-ended spin with a bounded handoff protocol, or
- initialize the host thread identity in a way that does not require the child to busy-wait on parent-side publication.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","pthread","resource-bounds","review"]}

- the parent only performs that store after `host_create` returns and after writing the thread id back to the caller (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1231-1252` and `1356-1380`).

Under normal scheduling this window is short, but there is no bound, no backoff, and no fallback park. If the creator thread is delayed under load, preempted in a bad spot, or stuck before the store, the child burns CPU in a pure spin loop.

That is a resource-bounds risk rather than a functional mismatch: it can turn thread creation pressure into avoidable CPU amplification.

Fix direction:
- replace the open-ended spin with a bounded handoff protocol, or
- initialize the host thread identity in a way that does not require the child to busy-wait on parent-side publication.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:27:55.648312390Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","pthread","resource-bounds","review"]}

 {"id":"bd-h5x","title":"EPIC: Remove glibc callthrough (raw syscalls + pthread primitives)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Goal: eliminate dependency on system glibc for core I/O/threading primitives when we claim replacement.

Deliverables:
- Raw syscall layer for Linux x86_64 (no libc::syscall).
- Futex-based pthread mutex primitives.
- Migrate ABIs to use the raw layer.

Acceptance:
- rg finds no libc pthread mutex call-through in the ‘replacement’ build profile.
- E2E smoke passes.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence

5. CI Gate Evidence

- `cargo test --all-targets`

-{"id":"bd-jqy64","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create and join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS plus propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create, join, detach, self, and equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}
+{"id":"bd-jqy64","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create and join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS plus propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create, join, detach, self, and equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:26:25.509781982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}

Add isolated harness subprocess coverage for tests/conformance/fixtures/wide_string_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity per mode, and fix any divergence exposed by the matrix path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.305846872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:43:59.323959467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:43:59.321768510Z","close_reason":"Added wide_string_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix in commit be2558b — dispatches all 16 fixture cases through harness conformance-matrix-case subprocess (mirrors search_ops pattern).  {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}
